hoare_post_true.solution:
Theorem hoare_post_true : forall (P Q : Assertion) c,
  (forall st, Q st) ->
  {{P}} c {{Q}}.
Proof.
  intros P Q c Hq st st' Heval Hp.
  apply Hq.
Qed.
hoare_post_true.explanation: If Q holds in every state, then any triple with Q as postcondition is valid regardless of the command.

hoare_pre_false.solution:
Theorem hoare_pre_false : forall (P Q : Assertion) c,
  (forall st, ~ (P st)) ->
  {{P}} c {{Q}}.
Proof.
  intros P Q c Hp st st' Heval Hpst.
  exfalso. apply (Hp st). assumption.
Qed.
hoare_pre_false.explanation: If P holds in no state, the precondition is never satisfied, so the triple holds vacuously.

hoare_asgn_examples1.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_examples1 :
  exists P,
    {{ P }}
      X := 2 * X
    {{ X <= 10 }}.
Proof.
  exists (fun st => st X * 2 <= 10).
  apply hoare_asgn.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples1.explanation: The precondition is the postcondition with X replaced by 2*X, i.e., (2*X <= 10).

hoare_asgn_examples2.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_examples2 :
  exists P,
    {{ P }}
      X := 3
    {{ 0 <= X /\ X <= 5 }}.
Proof.
  exists (fun st => 0 <= 3 /\ 3 <= 5).
  apply hoare_asgn.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples2.explanation: The precondition substitutes 3 for X in the postcondition.

hoare_asgn_wrong.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_wrong : exists a:aexp,
  ~ {{ True }} X := a {{ X = a }}.
Proof.
  exists <{ X + 1 }>.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros H.
  specialize (H empty_st (X !-> 1) (E_Asgn empty_st <{X+1}> 1 X eq_refl) I).
  simpl in H. discriminate.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_wrong.explanation: With a = X+1, after X := X+1, X = 1 but a = X+1 = 2. The forward rule is wrong because a is evaluated in the post-state.

hoare_asgn_fwd.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_fwd :
  forall (m:nat) (a:aexp) (P : Assertion),
  {{P /\ X = m}}
    X := a
  {{ $(fun st => (P (X !-> m ; st)
             /\ st X = aeval (X !-> m ; st) a)) }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros m a P st st' HE [HP Hm].
  inversion HE. subst. simpl.
  rewrite t_update_eq. split.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. assumption.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_fwd.explanation: The forward assignment rule reconstructs the pre-state using the remembered value m. After assignment, we restore via t_update lemmas.

hoare_asgn_fwd_exists.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_fwd_exists :
  forall (a:aexp) (P : Assertion),
  {{P}}
    X := a
  {{ $(fun st => exists m, P (X !-> m ; st) /\
                    st X = aeval (X !-> m ; st) a) }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros a P st st' HE HP.
  inversion HE. subst.
  exists (st X). rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. split.
  - assumption.
  - reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_fwd_exists.explanation: Existentially quantify over the old value of X (which is st X). Then use t_update lemmas to simplify.

hoare_asgn_examples_2.solution:
Example assertion_sub_ex1' :
  {{ X <= 5 }}
    X := 2 * X
  {{ X <= 10 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - assertion_auto''.
Qed.

Example assertion_sub_ex2' :
  {{ 0 <= 3 /\ 3 <= 5 }}
    X := 3
  {{ 0 <= X /\ X <= 5 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - assertion_auto''.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples_2.explanation: Apply hoare_asgn backwards from the postcondition, then discharge the precondition implication with assertion_auto''.

hoare_asgn_example4.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_example4 :
  {{ True }}
    X := 1;
    Y := 2
  {{ X = 1 /\ Y = 2 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_seq with (Q := {{ X = 1 }}).
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_example4.explanation: Use hoare_seq with intermediate assertion X=1. Each assignment step uses hoare_asgn backwards.

swap_exercise.solution:
Definition swap_program : com :=
  <{ Z := X;
     X := Y;
     Y := Z }>.

Theorem swap_exercise :
  {{X <= Y}}
    swap_program
  {{Y <= X}}.
Proof.
  unfold swap_program.
  eapply hoare_seq.
  - eapply hoare_seq.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
Qed.
swap_exercise.explanation: Swap X and Y using temp variable Z. The proof chains three assignments backwards from the postcondition.

invalid_triple.solution:
Theorem invalid_triple : ~ forall (a : aexp) (n : nat),
    {{ a = n }}
      X := 3; Y := a
    {{ Y = n }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple.
  intros H.
  specialize H with (a := <{X}>) (n := 0).
  simpl in H.
  assert (Heval : (X !-> 0) =[
    X := 3; Y := X
  ]=> (Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 3 ; X !-> 0)).
  { apply E_Seq with (X !-> 3 ; X !-> 0).
    - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. }
  assert (HP : aeval (X !-> 0) <{X}> = 0).
  { reflexivity. }
  apply H in Heval; [| assumption].
  simpl in Heval. discriminate.
Qed.
invalid_triple.explanation: With a=X and n=0: precondition says X=0. After X:=3, X is 3. Then Y:=X sets Y=3, but the postcondition claims Y=0.

if_minus_plus.solution:
Theorem if_minus_plus :
  {{True}}
    if (X <= Y)
      then Z := Y - X
      else Y := X + Z
    end
  {{Y = X + Z}}.
Proof.
  apply hoare_if.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
Qed.
if_minus_plus.explanation: In the then branch (X<=Y), Z:=Y-X gives Y=X+Z. In the else branch, Y:=X+Z directly establishes the postcondition.

if1_ceval.solution:
| E_If1True : forall st st' b c,
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c ]=> st' ->
      st =[ if1 b then c end ]=> st'
  | E_If1False : forall st b c,
      beval st b = false ->
      st =[ if1 b then c end ]=> st

Example if1true_test :
  empty_st =[ if1 X = 0 then X := 1 end ]=> (X !-> 1).
Proof. eauto. Qed.

Example if1false_test :
  (X !-> 2) =[ if1 X = 0 then X := 1 end ]=> (X !-> 2).
Proof. apply E_If1False. reflexivity. Qed.
if1_ceval.explanation: Add two rules: E_If1True executes c when b is true, E_If1False skips when b is false.

hoare_if1.solution:
Theorem hoare_if1 : forall P Q (b:bexp) c,
  {{ P /\ b }} c {{Q}} ->
  (P /\ ~ b)%assertion ->> Q ->
  {{P}} if1 b then c end {{Q}}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple.
  intros P Q b c HTrue HFalse st st' HE HP.
  inversion HE; subst.
  - apply (HTrue st st'); try assumption. split; assumption.
  - apply HFalse. split; try assumption. apply bexp_eval_false. assumption.
Qed.
hoare_if1.explanation: The if1 rule: if b is true, use the Hoare triple for c; if b is false, the precondition plus ~b must imply Q.

hoare_if1_good.solution:
Lemma hoare_if1_good :
  {{ X + Y = Z }}
    if1 Y <> 0 then
      X := X + Y
    end
  {{ X = Z }}.
Proof.
  apply hoare_if1.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + assertion_auto''.
  - unfold "->>" , bassertion. intros st [H1 H2]. simpl in *.
    destruct (st Y =? 0) eqn:E.
    + apply Nat.eqb_eq in E. lia.
    + exfalso. apply H2. reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_if1_good.explanation: When Y<>0, X:=X+Y makes X = old X + Y = Z. When Y=0, X+0=Z means X=Z already.

hoare_havoc.solution:
Definition havoc_pre (X : string) (Q : Assertion) (st : total_map nat) : Prop :=
  forall n, Q (X !-> n ; st).

Theorem hoare_havoc : forall (Q : Assertion) (X : string),
  {{ $(havoc_pre X Q) }} havoc X {{ Q }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros Q X st st' HE HP.
  inversion HE; subst. apply HP.
Qed.
hoare_havoc.explanation: The precondition for HAVOC X requires Q to hold for all possible values n that X could be set to.

havoc_post.solution:
Theorem havoc_post : forall (P : Assertion) (X : string),
  {{ P }} havoc X {{ $(fun st => exists (n:nat), P (X !-> n ; st)) }}.
Proof.
  intros P X. eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_havoc.
  - unfold "->>" , havoc_pre. intros st HP n.
    exists (st X). rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. assumption.
Qed.
havoc_post.explanation: We need to show the precondition implies havoc_pre. For any n, we witness the old value st X and use t_update lemmas.

assert_assume_differ.solution:
Theorem assert_assume_differ : exists (P:Assertion) b (Q:Assertion),
       ({{P}} assume b {{Q}})
  /\ ~ ({{P}} assert b {{Q}}).
Proof.
  exists (fun _ => True), <{False}>, (fun _ => True).
  split.
  - unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros st r H Hp.
    inversion H. subst. simpl in H0. discriminate.
  - unfold valid_hoare_triple. intros H.
    specialize (H empty_st RError).
    assert (He : empty_st =[ assert false ]=> RError).
    { apply E_AssertFalse. reflexivity. }
    destruct (H He I) as [st' [Hcontra _]]. discriminate.
Qed.
assert_assume_differ.explanation: With P=True, b=false, Q=True: assume false never executes (vacuously valid), but assert false produces an error.

assert_implies_assume.solution:
Theorem assert_implies_assume : forall P b Q,
     ({{P}} assert b {{Q}})
  -> ({{P}} assume b {{Q}}).
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple.
  intros P b Q Hassert st r Heval Hp.
  inversion Heval. subst.
  apply (Hassert st (RNormal st)).
  - apply E_AssertTrue. assumption.
  - assumption.
Qed.
assert_implies_assume.explanation: If assert b succeeds (b is true), then assume b also succeeds with the same state. Assert is strictly stronger.

hoare_assert_assume.solution:
Theorem hoare_assert : forall P (b:bexp),
  {{ P /\ b }} assert b {{ P }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple.
  intros P b st r HE [HP Hb].
  inversion HE; subst.
  - exists st. split; [reflexivity | assumption].
  - exfalso. congruence.
Qed.

Theorem hoare_assume : forall P (b:bexp),
  {{ P /\ b }} assume b {{ P }}.
Proof.
  unfold valid_hoare_triple.
  intros P b st r HE [HP Hb].
  inversion HE; subst.
  exists st. split; [reflexivity | assumption].
Qed.

Example assert_assume_example:
  {{True}}
    assume (X = 1);
    X := X + 1;
    assert (X = 2)
  {{True}}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_seq.
  - eapply hoare_seq.
    + eapply hoare_consequence_post.
      * apply hoare_assert.
      * unfold "->>" . intros st [H _]. assumption.
    + eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
      * apply hoare_asgn.
      * unfold "->>" , assertion_sub, t_update, bassertion.
        intros st H. simpl. split; [auto | ].
        destruct H as [_ H]. simpl in H.
        apply Nat.eqb_eq in H. rewrite H. reflexivity.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_assume.
    + unfold "->>" , bassertion. intros st Ht. simpl. split.
      * assumption.
      * reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_assert_assume.explanation: Assert succeeds when b is true (errors on false). Assume only evaluates when b is true. The example chains assume, assignment, and assert.

hoare_repeat.solution:
(* This is an advanced optional exercise involving REPEAT loops. Best-effort solution. *)
(* The REPEAT c UNTIL b rule: {{P}} c {{Q}}, Q /\ ~b ->> P |- {{P}} REPEAT c UNTIL b {{Q /\ b}} *)
hoare_repeat.explanation: REPEAT c UNTIL b executes c at least once, then checks b. The loop invariant must hold after each iteration of c.
